content.problemStatement:
This example demonstrates the usage of `tellg()` in C++, which tells the current position of the get pointer in an input stream.

- We have a text file named `sample.txt` containing:
```d
Hello, world!
This is a test file.
````

- Open the file using an `ifstream`
- Read the first line
- Use `tellg()` to find out the current read position
- Continue reading and observe how the position changes


**If the file pointer reaches the end of file (EOF), tellg() will return -1 to indicate that there is no more data to read.**
